# Network Coordination Concepts

> **Use boundary:** This file is written for research, ethics, detection, and policy analysis. It describes mechanisms at a conceptual level so they can be studied, criticised, or detected. It is not an execution guide for manipulating platforms, audiences, investors, advertisers, or creators.


Coordination research often looks for repeated similar actions by multiple accounts within narrow time windows. The point is not to prove intent from one coincidence, but to identify patterns where repeated synchronization becomes unlikely to be accidental.

## Non-operational indicators

- Same URL shared by many pages/accounts close together.
- Same caption or hashtag sequence repeated.
- Same asset posted with minor edits.
- Dense clusters of accounts that repeatedly interact with each other.
- Accounts created in similar windows.
- Activity concentrated around launch windows or controversies.

## Interpretation caution

Coordination is not always inauthentic. Fans, activists, open-source communities, emergency responders, and real movements can coordinate authentically. The analytical problem is to determine whether coordination is disclosed, independent, materially deceptive, or platform-manipulative.

## Source notes

- **Rogers & Righetti (2025) — Coordinated inauthentic behaviour on Facebook? A typology of manufactured attention** (Academic / open-access). Defines manufactured attention as artificial audience fabrication through coordinated behaviour; surfaces advertising networks, gambling/cyber-scam promotion, hijacked public groups, media groups, and political/activist coordination. URL: https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/29768624251369784
